channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Five Gemma-4 models, one accelerator: what porting E2B 31B to AWS Inferentia2 taught me
item.description: I ported the whole Gemma-4 family — E2B, E4B, 12B, 31B, and the 26B-A4B MoE — to run on AWS Inferentia2. Each has its own write-up in this series; this is the map. What's shared, what's different,...

item.title: Show DEV: I built a lightweight, extensible Web Page Builder using React 🚀
item.description: Hey DEV Community! 👋 I wanted to share a project I've been working on recently: Pepsi Page Builder. It’s an open-source, lightweight web page builder designed to help users create responsive...

item.title: HackTheBox : Void Whispers Writeup
item.description: Summary The "Void Whispers" mail-settings panel passes the user-supplied sendMailPath field directly into shell_exec("which $sendMailPath") with no escaping. The app only filters literal...

item.title: Generator Internals: Why They Are Lazy and What That Actually Means
item.description: Tracing a generator's execution reveals a fundamentally different way Python can run code. A generator function looks like a regular function but behaves in a fundamentally different way....

item.title: Godot 4 hitbox and hurtbox the easy way (damage, teams, i-frames, knockback)
item.description: Almost every action game needs the same thing: something that deals damage, and something that takes it. In Godot 4 the raw tools are Area2D + CollisionShape2D, but wiring them into a real hit is...

item.title: Porting Gemma-4 12B (the encoder-free multimodal one) to AWS Inferentia2
item.description: The middle child of the series. The 12B is the first Gemma-4 that's neither a MatFormer "effective" model (E2B/E4B) nor a giant (31B/26B) — but it has its own two surprises: it's packaged as a...

item.title: My Inferentia port matched its reference token-for-token — and still output garbage
item.description: I ported Google's biggest open Gemma-4 — the 30-billion-parameter dense model — to AWS Inferentia2. The compiled model passed my strictest check: its output was token-for-token identical to the...

item.title: Porting a 128-expert MoE (Gemma-4 26B-A4B) to AWS Inferentia2 — where every rank weighted the wrong experts
item.description: Of the five Gemma-4 models I ported to AWS Inferentia2, the 26B-A4B was the one that was supposed to be impossible: a Mixture of Experts. It compiled on the first try — then produced nothing. The...

item.title: Five Gemma-4 models, one accelerator: what porting E2B 31B to AWS Inferentia2 taught me
item.description: I ported the whole Gemma-4 family — E2B, E4B, 12B, 31B, and the 26B-A4B MoE — to run on AWS Inferentia2. Each has its own write-up in this series; this is the map. What's shared, what's different,...

item.title: The Silent Crash: How Hallucinated Tool Breaks AI Agents
item.description: There isn't one version of this failure, there are three distinct patterns, and they cause different kinds of damage. Calling a tool that doesn't exist. The agent invokes a function name that was...

item.title: Trump is selling high-speed access to his market-moving Truth Social posts
item.description: Trump Media, the company behind Truth Social, is selling Wall Street faster access to the "most market-moving" posts on the US President's social media platform. On Thursday, Trump Media announced...

item.title: The Download: perimenopause misinformation and China’s latest AI leap
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it....

item.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
item.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

item.title: E.U. Orders Google to Open Android Mic, Camera and Screen to Rival AI Assistants
item.description: The European Commission on Thursday ordered Google to give rival AI assistants the same reach into Android that Gemini already has: the camera, the microphone, whatever is on screen, a wake word...

item.title: The Race to Field Military Autonomy Is On, Can Trusted Information Infrastructure Keep Pace?
item.description: Military forces are under increasing pressure to field autonomous capabilities faster than ever before. Across the U.S., UK, and NATO, new investment, evolving defense strategies, and accelerated...

item.title: Details of Alan Turing’s Voice Encryption System
item.description: Really interesting piece of cryptographic history: In November 2023, a large cache of his wartime papers—nicknamed the “Bayley papers”—was auctioned in London for almost half a million U.S....

item.title: The war on ‘woke science’ comes for space research
item.description: The Trump administration is waging a culture war on science, and the latest salvo is in the form of a dry, bureaucratic proposal from the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) that could threaten...

item.title: Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
item.description: Armenia has held a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in a detention center since June 28, on a U.S. extradition request for a REvil ransomware suspect named Aleksandr Ermakov. His wife,...

item.title: God Of War TV series is recasting Kratos
item.description: Amazon's upcoming God Of War show has hit a major snag - it's now on the hunt for a new Kratos, after an on-set injury put its current lead actor out of commission. Sons of Anarchy star Ryan Hurst...

item.title: San Francisco Demands Apple and Google Delete AI ‘Nudify’ Apps From App Stores
item.description: The City Attorney’s Office sent the tech giants cease-and-desist letters this week telling them to stop profiting from 13 “face-swap” apps that are overwhelmingly used to target women and girls.

item.title: A scorecard for the AI age
item.description: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenaAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

item.title: There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it.
item.description: Perimenopause has entered the chat. Perimenopause—and its better-known relative, menopause—used to be considered taboo. Not anymore, thanks at least in part to TV doctors and social media...

item.title: A Humanoid Company Backed by Eric Trump Is Preparing Its Robots for War
item.description: The CEO of Foundation Future Industries, which counts the president’s son as its chief strategy adviser, tells WIRED it’s exploring some “kinetic things.”

item.title: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
item.description: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people...

item.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
item.description: ACR Stealer, an infostealer in circulation since 2024, is walking out of enterprise networks with saved browser passwords, live session tokens, PDFs, Microsoft 365 documents, and files from synced...

item.title: New GoSerpent Malware Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Diplomats for Espionage
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented malware called GoSerpent that has been put to use in cyber attacks targeting entities in Southeast Asia since late 2025 with a...

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly patched security flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV)...

item.title: Why Apple Sued OpenAI, New York Takes on Data Centers, and What to Know about Cyclosporiasis
item.description: On today’s Uncanny Valley, we unpack OpenAI’s ongoing drama, both legal and reputational, and whether these developments could further hurt the company—particularly in its fight against Anthropic.

item.title: HP fined 1.4 billion rupees for “cartelization” of ink cartridges, toner, PCs
item.description: Resellers threatened to ditch HP printing supplies for counterfeits.

item.title: Fortnite is getting a bunch of AI-powered ‘personas’
item.description: Get ready for more AI characters in Fortnite. Developer Epic Games is going to let Fortnite creators publish experiences featuring characters with AI-powered voices starting on July 30th, and...
